title: Operation & Maintenance Engineer
description:
An EPC Solar PV System Integrator providing technologies solutions in Engineering, Design, Procurement, Construction, Testing and Commissioning, Operations and Maintenance of Solar Energy Systems from concept to completion.

Job Scope:

  • Oversee the daily monitoring of inverter data, system yield, and overall solar plant performance. 
  • This role is ideal for an engineering professional who enjoys working with numbers, data analytics, and performance optimization. 
  • The candidate will be responsible for analyzing system data, identifying potential issues, and ensuring optimal performance of solar PV systems.
  • The candidate will be at the forefront of solar energy innovation and sustainability, work with a team of experts in renewable energy and data analytics, contribute to optimizing clean energy solutions through data-driven decision-making.

Responsibilities:

  • Monitor and analyze daily inverter data to track energy yield and system status.
  • Identify underperforming systems and troubleshoot performance issues.
  • Develop and maintain real-time dashboards and reports to track key performance indicators (KPIs).
  • Work with field technicians to diagnose and resolve system faults or anomalies.
  • Conduct data-driven performance analysis to optimize system efficiency.
  • Assist in developing predictive maintenance strategies using historical data trends.
  • Generate performance reports and provide insights to the operations and management team.
  • Collaborate with the engineering team to improve system design and software analytics tools.
  • Stay updated on the latest solar technology trends, analytics tools, and industry best practices.

Requirements:

  • Diploma or Degree in Engineering (Electrical, Mechanical, Renewable Energy, Electronic, IT or related field).
  • Passion for data analysis, numbers, and performance monitoring.
  • Experience with solar PV systems, inverters, and monitoring software is a plus.
  • Proficiency in data visualization tools (Excel, Python, Power BI, or similar).
  • Strong analytical skills with a keen eye for identifying trends and anomalies.
  • Ability to communicate findings effectively to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
  • Detail-oriented, proactive, and able to work independently in a fast-paced environment.

Others:

  • Work location: Central region, Singapore (Near MRT)
  • Working hours: 8.30am to 6.00pm, 5 days’ work week
